title: Segment-Level Attribution for Selective Learning of Long Reasoning Traces

methodology.approach: Segment-level attribution using Integrated Gradient (IG) attribution
methodology.key_innovation: Direct calculation of each token's influence on correct answer prediction
methodology.problem_addressed: Redundant segments in long Chain-of-Thought (CoT) reasoning traces
methodology.metrics: Normalized strength across segments with low strength and high consistency

findings: Unimportant segments cluster into distinctive redundant patterns
Incorrect CoTs typically have more segments and tokens than correct CoTs for the same queries
Existing methods (token-level analysis, perplexity, entropy) have limitations including false positives and false negatives
Verbosity without substance actively degrades reasoning performance as CoT length increases
Training LLMs on verbose CoT supervision with sparse positive contribution leads to models learning to imitate redundant behaviors
